body:
**Actionable comments posted: 1** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> packages/ui-chess-elements/src/__tests__/ChessBoard.test.ts (1)
> > `6-15`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ > > **`performance.now()` をモックしてテストを決定論化してください。** > > Line 9-15 のセットアップで時刻依存関数の固定化がなく、将来的な非決定要因になります。`beforeEach` で `performance.now()` をモックしてください。 > >
> 🔧 提案差分 > > ```diff > beforeEach(() => { > document.body.innerHTML = ""; > + vi.spyOn(performance, "now").mockReturnValue(0); > }); > > afterEach(() => { > vi.restoreAllMocks(); > }); > ``` >
> > > As per coding guidelines `**/*.test.{ts,tsx}`: Write deterministic tests that do not depend on environment factors like time; mock `performance.now()` and other time-dependent functions. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@packages/ui-chess-elements/src/__tests__/ChessBoard.test.ts` around lines 6 - > 15, The tests are non-deterministic because performance.now() is not mocked; in > beforeEach (the existing beforeEach block) add a mock for performance.now() > (e.g., use vi.spyOn(performance, "now").mockReturnValue()) so > time-dependent code is deterministic, and ensure you restore the spy in > afterEach (alongside vi.restoreAllMocks()) so the mock doesn't leak; reference > the beforeEach/afterEach setup and vi.restoreAllMocks() when making the change. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md (1)
`8-8`: **運用文書として断定表現を少し緩めるとより安全です。** Line 8 の `without manual intervention` は運用現場で誤解されやすいため、最小限介入を前提にした表現へ寄せるとよいです。
✍️ 提案差分 ```diff -AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention. +AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles with minimal manual intervention. ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In @.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md at line 8, 現在の文中の断定的な表現 "without manual intervention" をより安全な表現に置き換えてください — 例えば "with minimal manual intervention" や "with minimal human oversight" のように、完全自動を否定して最小限の介入を前提とする表現に修正します。該当箇所は説明文中のそのフレーズなので、当該文を該当フレーズを用いて検索して置換し、文脈に合う自然な日本語訳（例: 「最小限の手動対応を想定して」）に更新してください。不要に断定的な語調が残らないよう、1〜2案で表現を微調整してコミットしてください。 ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@packages/ui-chess/src/elements.ts`: - Around line 137-142: ファイル内でプライベートフィールド `_focusedIndex` が二重に宣言されているため TypeScript がコンパイルエラーになるので、重複する宣言ブロック（＠state() デコレータ付きの private _focusedIndex = 0;）を削除して `_focusedIndex` の宣言を一箇所に統一してください。該当する重複宣言はコメントにあるブロック（“2026 Zenith Tier: Roving Tabindex state.” と `@state`() が付いたもの）なので、そのブロックを削除し、残した宣言が `@state`() を保持しているか参照箇所（_focusedIndex を参照するメソッド/レンダリング）に影響がないか軽く確認してください。 --- Outside diff comments: In `@packages/ui-chess-elements/src/__tests__/ChessBoard.test.ts`: - Around line 6-15: The tests are non-deterministic because performance.now() is not mocked; in beforeEach (the existing beforeEach block) add a mock for performance.now() (e.g., use vi.spyOn(performance, "now").mockReturnValue()) so time-dependent code is deterministic, and ensure you restore the spy in afterEach (alongside vi.restoreAllMocks()) so the mock doesn't leak; reference the beforeEach/afterEach setup and vi.restoreAllMocks() when making the change. --- Nitpick comments: In @.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md: - Line 8: 現在の文中の断定的な表現 "without manual intervention" をより安全な表現に置き換えてください — 例えば "with minimal manual intervention" や "with minimal human oversight" のように、完全自動を否定して最小限の介入を前提とする表現に修正します。該当箇所は説明文中のそのフレーズなので、当該文を該当フレーズを用いて検索して置換し、文脈に合う自然な日本語訳（例: 「最小限の手動対応を想定して」）に更新してください。不要に断定的な語調が残らないよう、1〜2案で表現を微調整してコミットしてください。 ```
---
ℹ️ Review info **Configuration used**: Path: .coderabbit.yaml **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between f57763948a88eadc2263dfd6b798b784cc2ba20e and 2483a9872e507efe12806c6153fa3dc67b423bdb.
📒 Files selected for processing (5) * `.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md` * `docs/en/ZENITH_STANDARD.md` * `packages/i18n-common/locales/ja.json` * `packages/ui-chess-elements/src/__tests__/ChessBoard.test.ts` * `packages/ui-chess/src/elements.ts`

## Code Review

このプルリクエストは、エンジンレジストリとコアライフサイクルの堅牢性を高めるための重要な変更を含んでいます。特に、i18nの品質向上やアクセシビリティ対応は素晴らしい改善です。
レビューの結果、いくつかの修正点が見つかりました。
- `elements.ts`におけるプロパティの重複定義
- `ja.json`における翻訳文の末尾記号の不統一
- テストファイル内の未使用のインポート
- スキル定義ドキュメントの記述の曖昧さ
これらの点を修正することで、コードの品質がさらに向上します。詳細については、各コメントをご確認ください。

**Actionable comments posted: 9** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (3)
> >
> packages/core/src/protocol/ProtocolValidator.ts (1)
> > `81-125`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **循環判定が広すぎて、非循環の共有参照も誤検知します。** > > 現在の実装は探索全体で訪問済みを保持するため、循環していない「同一オブジェクトの再利用」も拒否されます。 > 再帰スタック単位で管理するため、復帰時に `visited.delete(input)` を行ってください。 > > >
> 修正案（再帰スタックベースの循環検知） > > ```diff > if (recursive && typeof input === "object" && input !== null) { > // 2026: 循環参照チェック (Zenith 6.4) > if (visited.has(input)) { > const i18nKey = createI18nKey("engine.errors.nestedTooDeep"); > throw new EngineError({ > code: EngineErrorCode.SECURITY_ERROR, > message: `Circular reference detected in ${context}.`, > i18nKey, > }); > } > visited.add(input); > - > - if (Array.isArray(input)) { > - for (const item of input) { > - ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > - item, > - context, > - true, > - allowSemicolon, > - depth + 1, > - visited, > - ); > - } > - } else { > - for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(input)) { > - // キー自体もインジェクションチェックの対象とする > - ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > - key, > - `${context} key`, > - false, > - allowSemicolon, > - depth + 1, > - visited, > - ); > - // 値を再帰的にチェック > - ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > - value, > - context, > - true, > - allowSemicolon, > - depth + 1, > - visited, > - ); > - } > - } > + try { > + if (Array.isArray(input)) { > + for (const item of input) { > + ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > + item, > + context, > + true, > + allowSemicolon, > + depth + 1, > + visited, > + ); > + } > + } else { > + for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(input)) { > + // キー自体もインジェクションチェックの対象とする > + ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > + key, > + `${context} key`, > + false, > + allowSemicolon, > + depth + 1, > + visited, > + ); > + // 値を再帰的にチェック > + ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection( > + value, > + context, > + true, > + allowSemicolon, > + depth + 1, > + visited, > + ); > + } > + } > + } finally { > + visited.delete(input); > + } > } > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@packages/core/src/protocol/ProtocolValidator.ts` around lines 81 - 125, The > circular-reference check in ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection uses a global > visited Set across the whole traversal and thus misclassifies non-circular > shared references as cycles; change it to track visited per-recursion-stack by > removing the current input from visited when unwinding (i.e., after processing > children, call visited.delete(input)) so that shared but non-circular objects > are allowed; update the logic around the visited.add(input) / traversal of > Array.isArray(input) and Object.entries(input) to ensure visited.delete(input) > always runs on exit (including in exceptional paths). > ``` > >
> >
>
> packages/core/src/adapters/BaseAdapter.ts (1)
> > `130-142`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **SRI バイパス経路が残っており、セキュリティ方針と衝突しています。** > > Line 140-142 で `__unsafeNoSRI: true` を許可しているため、ブック資産を SRI なしで読み込めます。ここは `sri` 必須に寄せるべきです。 > > >
> 🔒 提案差分 > > ```diff > - if (!asset.sri && !asset.__unsafeNoSRI) { > + if (!asset.sri) { > const i18nKey = createI18nKey("engine.errors.sriMismatch"); > throw new EngineError({ > code: EngineErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR, > message: `Engine Adapter "${this.id}": Book asset "${asset.id}" requires an SRI hash for security.`, > i18nKey, > engineId: this.id, > }); > } > > - const config: IEngineSourceConfig = asset.sri > - ? { url: asset.url, type: "asset", sri: asset.sri } > - : { url: asset.url, type: "asset", __unsafeNoSRI: true }; > + const config: IEngineSourceConfig = { > + url: asset.url, > + type: "asset", > + sri: asset.sri, > + }; > ``` >
> > As per coding guidelines: `**/*.{ts,tsx}`: Enforce SRI hash verification for all resource loads. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@packages/core/src/adapters/BaseAdapter.ts` around lines 130 - 142, The branch > that permits an SRI bypass (using __unsafeNoSRI) must be removed: ensure Book > asset loads always require asset.sri by eliminating the ternary that sets > __unsafeNoSRI and instead construct IEngineSourceConfig with sri present (e.g., > { url: asset.url, type: "asset", sri: asset.sri }) — keep the prior validation > that throws an EngineError when asset.sri is missing and remove any code paths > that set __unsafeNoSRI or rely on it (references: asset.sri, __unsafeNoSRI, > IEngineSourceConfig, EngineError). > ``` > >
> >
>
> packages/core/src/bridge/EngineFacade.ts (1)
> > `409-460`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **`dispose()` の同時呼び出しで二重破棄が発生し得ます。** > > `isAlreadyDisposed` を Line 410-412 で先に評価しているだけなので、並行 `dispose()` が入ると両方が `adapter.stop()/adapter.dispose()` を実行できます。`disposePromise` による単一実行ガードが必要です。 > > >
> 🛡️ 提案差分 > > ```diff > export class EngineFacade< > @@ > > implements IEngine { > + private disposePromise: Promise | null = null; > @@ > async dispose(): Promise { > - const id = this.id; > - const isAlreadyDisposed = > - this.adapter.status === "disposed" || > - this.adapter.status === "terminated"; > + if (this.disposePromise) return this.disposePromise; > + > + this.disposePromise = (async () => { > + const id = this.id; > + const isAlreadyDisposed = > + this.adapter.status === "disposed" || > + this.adapter.status === "terminated"; > @@ > - if (this.ownAdapter && !isAlreadyDisposed) { > + if (this.ownAdapter && !isAlreadyDisposed) { > try { > // 2026: Graceful Shutdown (quit 送信) > await this.adapter.stop(); > @@ > - } > - } > + } > + })(); > + > + try { > + await this.disposePromise; > + } finally { > + this.disposePromise = null; > + } > } > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@packages/core/src/bridge/EngineFacade.ts` around lines 409 - 460, Concurrent > calls to dispose() can run adapter.stop()/adapter.dispose() twice because > isAlreadyDisposed is only read once; add a single-execution guard property > (e.g., this.disposePromise or this._disposePromise) in the EngineFacade.dispose > implementation: at the top, if a dispose promise exists return/await it; > otherwise set this.disposePromise = (async () => { ...existing disposal logic... > })(); ensure all existing cleanup (unsubscribers, listeners, activeTaskStop, > consentDeferred, loaderProvider revoke) runs inside that async wrapper, and on > completion or error clear the disposePromise (or leave fulfilled promise) so > subsequent calls wait for the same promise rather than re-entering > adapter.stop()/adapter.dispose(). Use the existing symbols adapter.stop, > adapter.dispose, activeTaskStop, consentDeferred, loaderProvider, > adapterUnsubscribers and statusListeners/resultListeners/telemetryListeners to > locate where to wrap and set the promise. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (7)
packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/deepMerge.test.ts (2)
`5-38`: **型キャストパターンについて** `as unknown as typeof target` のキャストが複数箇所で使用されています。テストコードでは許容範囲ですが、`deepMerge` 関数のシグネチャを見直すか、テスト用のヘルパー関数を作成することで、キャストの繰り返しを減らせる可能性があります。 現状のキャストは `any` を使用しておらず、ガイドラインには適合しているため、優先度は低いです。
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/deepMerge.test.ts` around lines 5 - 38, Tests repeatedly use the pattern "as unknown as typeof target" when calling deepMerge; reduce this duplication by either (A) loosening or making a proper generic/type-safe signature for the deepMerge function (e.g., update deepMerge(target: T, source?: Partial): T) so tests can call it without casts, or (B) add a small test helper like callDeepMerge(t: T, s?: Partial) that wraps deepMerge and provides correct generics so tests (the specs in deepMerge.test.ts) can pass source values without the double cast; update the existing tests to use the chosen approach and remove the repeated "as unknown as typeof target" casts. ```
--- `40-50`: **プロトタイプ汚染テストに再帰的なケースを追加してください。** 現在のテストはトップレベルの `__proto__` のみを検証していますが、ネストされたオブジェクト内での汚染や `constructor.prototype` を介した汚染についてもテストが必要です。
♻️ 再帰的なプロトタイプ汚染テストの追加案 ```diff it("should prevent prototype pollution", () => { const target = {} as Record; const source = JSON.parse('{"__proto__": {"polluted": true}}') as Record< string, unknown >; deepMerge(target, source); expect((target as Record).polluted).toBeUndefined(); expect(({} as Record).polluted).toBeUndefined(); }); + + it("should prevent nested prototype pollution", () => { + const target = { nested: {} } as Record; + const source = JSON.parse( + '{"nested": {"__proto__": {"nestedPolluted": true}}}' + ) as Record; + + deepMerge(target, source); + expect(({} as Record).nestedPolluted).toBeUndefined(); + }); + + it("should prevent constructor.prototype pollution", () => { + const target = {} as Record; + const source = JSON.parse( + '{"constructor": {"prototype": {"constructorPolluted": true}}}' + ) as Record; + + deepMerge(target, source); + expect(({} as Record).constructorPolluted).toBeUndefined(); + }); ```
Based on learnings: 「Include recursive object traversal in injection validation tests.」
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/deepMerge.test.ts` around lines 40 - 50, Add recursive prototype-pollution cases to the deepMerge test: extend the existing test for deepMerge by adding sources that include nested "__proto__" keys (e.g., { a: { "__proto__": { polluted: true } } }) and a payload that targets constructor.prototype (e.g., { a: { constructor: { prototype: { polluted: true } } } }), call deepMerge(target, source) for each, and assert that neither target.polluted nor ({}).polluted become defined; use the same test harness and assertions in deepMerge.test.ts and keep test names descriptive (e.g., "should prevent recursive prototype pollution via __proto__" and "should prevent prototype pollution via constructor.prototype"). ```
packages/core/src/protocol/__tests__/ProtocolValidator.test.ts (1)
`97-120`: **エラー契約を固定するため、`code` も併せて検証するとより堅牢です。** `i18nKey` だけだと別種エラーでも通る余地があるため、`EngineErrorCode.SECURITY_ERROR` も期待値に含めるのを推奨します。
テスト強化案 ```diff import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeAll, afterAll } from "vitest"; import { ProtocolValidator } from "../ProtocolValidator.js"; +import { EngineErrorCode } from "../../types.js"; @@ expect(() => ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection(circular, "context", true), ).toThrow( - expect.objectContaining({ i18nKey: "engine.errors.nestedTooDeep" }), + expect.objectContaining({ + i18nKey: "engine.errors.nestedTooDeep", + code: EngineErrorCode.SECURITY_ERROR, + }), ); @@ expect(() => ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection(deep, "context", true), ).toThrow( - expect.objectContaining({ i18nKey: "engine.errors.nestedTooDeep" }), + expect.objectContaining({ + i18nKey: "engine.errors.nestedTooDeep", + code: EngineErrorCode.SECURITY_ERROR, + }), ); ```
As per coding guidelines, "Write deterministic tests that do not depend on environment factors like time; mock `performance.now()` and other time-dependent functions."
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/protocol/__tests__/ProtocolValidator.test.ts` around lines 97 - 120, Update the two tests that call ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection to assert the error's code as well as i18nKey: expect.toThrow(expect.objectContaining({ i18nKey: "engine.errors.nestedTooDeep", code: EngineErrorCode.SECURITY_ERROR })); reference ProtocolValidator.assertNoInjection and EngineErrorCode in the test file so the expected error contract is strict, and make the tests deterministic by mocking time-dependent functions (e.g., stub performance.now()) before invoking the assertions. ```
packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/ConfigValidator.test.ts (2)
`1-66`: **テスト決定性ルールに対して `performance.now()` モックが未設定です** このファイルは時間依存ロジックを直接触っていなくても、共通ルールとして `performance.now()` を固定しておくと将来の揺らぎを防げます。 As per coding guidelines `**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js}`: "Ensure tests are deterministic by mocking `performance.now()`."
追加例 ```diff -import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; +import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from "vitest"; + +beforeEach(() => { + vi.spyOn(performance, "now").mockReturnValue(0); +}); + +afterEach(() => { + vi.restoreAllMocks(); +}); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/ConfigValidator.test.ts` around lines 1 - 66, Add a deterministic mock for performance.now() in this test file so timing can't introduce flakiness: in the ConfigValidator.test.ts file (where describe/it and normalizeAndValidateSources are used) stub or spy on global performance.now (e.g., using vi.stubGlobal or vi.spyOn(globalThis, "performance", ...)) to return a fixed numeric value in a setup block (beforeAll/beforeEach) and restore the original in teardown (afterAll/afterEach), ensuring all tests in the file run with a stable performance.now(). ```
--- `6-65`: **Injection 検証に再帰オブジェクト走査ケースが不足しています** `sources` にネスト構造を含めた payload で、再帰的に不正文字列を検出できるかのケースを1本追加することを推奨します。 Based on learnings "Applies to **/*.test.{ts,tsx} : Include recursive object traversal in injection validation tests."
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/utils/__tests__/ConfigValidator.test.ts` around lines 6 - 65, Add a test inside the "normalizeAndValidateSources" describe that passes a config with nested sources (e.g., sources: { main: { url: "good.js" }, nested: { level1: { level2: { url: "" } } } }) and call normalizeAndValidateSources(registrySources?, config, "test-id"), then assert it throws due to injection detection; use expect(() => normalizeAndValidateSources(...)).toThrow(...) and assert the thrown error/message contains the malicious substring (e.g., "" } } } }) and call normalizeAndValidateSources(registrySources?, config, "test-id"), then assert it throws due to injection detection; use expect(() => normalizeAndValidateSources(...)).toThrow(...) and assert the thrown error/message contains the malicious substring (e.g., "

**Actionable comments posted: 2**
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
packages/core/src/utils/ConfigValidator.ts (1)
`20-36`: **型アサーションの重複を簡素化できます** Line 23で `as NonNullable` を適用していますが、この時点では `sources.main` の存在が未検証です。また、Line 36で同じ型への再アサーションは冗長です。 検証後に一度だけ型を絞り込む方がより型安全なアプローチです。
♻️ 型アサーションを検証後に移動する提案 ```diff export function normalizeAndValidateSources( registrySources: IEngineConfig["sources"] | null | undefined, config: IEngineConfig, defaultEngineId: string, ): NonNullable { const sources = { ...(registrySources || {}), ...(config.sources || {}), - } as NonNullable; + }; if (!sources.main) { const engineId = config.id || defaultEngineId; throw new EngineError({ code: EngineErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR, message: `[createEngine] Engine "${engineId}" requires a "main" source, but it was not found in the registry or config.`, engineId, i18nKey: createI18nKey("factory.requiresMainSource"), i18nParams: { id: engineId }, }); } - return sources as NonNullable; + // sources.main の存在を検証済みのため、型アサーションは安全 + return sources as NonNullable; } ```
なお、TypeScriptの型推論では `if (!sources.main)` のガード後に自動的に型を絞り込めないため、最終的なアサーションは必要ですが、検証前のLine 23のアサーションは削除可能です。
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/core/src/utils/ConfigValidator.ts` around lines 20 - 36, Remove the premature type assertion on the sources variable and only assert after validating presence of main: declare sources = { ...(registrySources || {}), ...(config.sources || {}) } (no as NonNullable...), keep the if (!sources.main) validation as-is that throws EngineError (use engineId, EngineErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR and createI18nKey exactly as in the diff), then at the end return sources as NonNullable; this moves the single necessary assertion to after the runtime check and eliminates the redundant/unsafe assertion at declaration. ```
